en.summary: The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) described a series of visions he saw, each depicting how a specific good deed -- such as honoring parents, ablution, dhikr, prayer, fasting, ghusl, Hajj, maintaining family ties, charity, enjoining good, good character, fear of God, deceased children, hope in God, tears of humility, good opinion of God, sending blessings upon the Prophet, and the shahada -- intervenes to save a person from trials of death, the grave, and the Day of Judgment. This famous hadith, narrated by the companion Abd al-Rahman ibn Samura at a gathering with the Prophet in Medina, served as comprehensive moral instruction cataloguing the salvific power of righteous deeds in the Hereafter.

key_terms.عجائب: Astonishing things, wonders -- referring to the Prophet's visionary experiences
key_terms.ملك الموت: The Angel of Death (Azrael), who takes souls at the time of death
key_terms.عذاب القبر: Punishment of the grave, a trial faced between death and resurrection
key_terms.الصراط: The Path/Bridge over Hell that all must cross on the Day of Judgment
key_terms.الزبانية: Angels of punishment who guard Hell
key_terms.موازين: Scales of deeds, weighed on the Day of Judgment
key_terms.الأمر بالمعروف والنهي عن المنكر: Enjoining good and forbidding evil, a fundamental Islamic duty

en.seo_question: What did the Prophet Muhammad say about the good deeds that save people on the Day of Judgment?

ur.summary: نبی اکرم صلی الله علیہ وآلہ وسلم نے عجائب بیان فرمائے جو آپ نے دیکھے، ہر ایک میں دکھایا گیا کہ کس طرح ایک نیک عمل موت، قبر اور قیامت کی آزمائشوں سے بچاتا ہے۔
ur.seo_question: قیامت کے دن نیک اعمال انسان کو کیسے بچاتے ہیں؟

tr.summary: Hz. Peygamber (s.a.v.s.) gördüğü hayret verici şeyleri anlattı; her birinde çeşitli amellerin ölüm, kabir ve kıyamet sınavlarından nasıl kurtardığı gösterilmiştir.
tr.seo_question: Kıyamet gününde hangi ameller insanı kurtarır?

fa.summary: پیامبر اکرم (صلی الله علیه وآله وسلم) عجایبی را که دیده بودند وصف فرمودند، که در هر کدام نشان داده شد چگونه اعمال نیک انسان را از آزمایش‌های مرگ، قبر و قیامت نجات می‌دهد.
fa.seo_question: چه اعمالی انسان را در روز قیامت نجات می‌دهند؟

id.summary: Nabi (saw) menggambarkan serangkaian penglihatan menakjubkan, masing-masing menunjukkan bagaimana amal saleh tertentu menyelamatkan seseorang dari ujian kematian, kubur, dan Hari Kiamat.
id.seo_question: Amal saleh apa saja yang menyelamatkan manusia di Hari Kiamat?

bn.summary: নবী (সা.) তাঁর দেখা বিস্ময়কর দৃশ্যগুলি বর্ণনা করেছেন, প্রতিটিতে দেখানো হয়েছে কিভাবে সৎকর্ম মৃত্যু, কবর ও কিয়ামতের পরীক্ষা থেকে রক্ষা করে।
bn.seo_question: কিয়ামতের দিন কোন সৎকর্মগুলো মানুষকে রক্ষা করবে?

es.summary: El Profeta (la paz sea con él y su familia) describió una serie de visiones asombrosas, cada una mostrando cómo un acto virtuoso específico salva a la persona de las pruebas de la muerte, la tumba y el Día del Juicio.
es.seo_question: ¿Qué buenas obras salvan a las personas en el Día del Juicio según el Profeta?

fr.summary: Le Prophète (paix et bénédictions sur lui et sa famille) a décrit une série de visions étonnantes, chacune montrant comment un acte vertueux spécifique sauve la personne des épreuves de la mort, de la tombe et du Jour du Jugement.
fr.seo_question: Quelles bonnes actions sauvent les gens le Jour du Jugement selon le Prophète ?

de.summary: Der Prophet (Friede sei mit ihm und seiner Familie) beschrieb eine Reihe erstaunlicher Visionen, die jeweils zeigen, wie eine bestimmte gute Tat einen Menschen vor den Prüfungen des Todes, des Grabes und des Jüngsten Tages rettet.
de.seo_question: Welche guten Taten retten die Menschen am Tag des Gerichts laut dem Propheten?

ru.summary: Пророк (мир ему и его семье) описал серию удивительных видений, каждое из которых показывает, как конкретное благое дело спасает человека от испытаний смерти, могилы и Судного дня.
ru.seo_question: Какие благие деяния спасут человека в Судный день по словам Пророка?

zh.summary: 先知（愿主福安之及其家属）描述了他所见的一系列奇异景象，每个景象展示了一项特定善行如何在死亡、坟墓和审判日的考验中拯救一个人。
zh.seo_question: 先知说哪些善行能在审判日拯救人们？

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا الشَّيْخُ الْجَلِيلُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مُوسَى ابْنِ بَابَوَيْهِ الْقُمِّيِّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا صَالِحُ بْنُ عِيسَى الْعِجْلِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ ابْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الصَّلْتِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بُكَيْرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ عَبَّادٍ الْمُهَلَّبِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ هِلَالِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ جُدْعَانَ عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ابْنِ سَمُرَةَ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: كُنَّا عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ يَوْمًا فَقَالَ إِنِّي رَأَيْتُ الْبَارِحَةَ عَجَائِبَ

narrators.name_ar: أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مُوسَى ابْنِ بَابَوَيْهِ الْقُمِّيِّ
narrators.name_en: Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Musa ibn Babawayh al-Qummi
narrators.known_identity: Shaykh al-Saduq (d. 381 AH), major Shia hadith compiler and author of Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih

narrators.ambiguity_note: Identified as Salih ibn Isa al-Ijli based on the name pattern; less commonly cited in Shia hadith collections
narrators.known_identity: Salih ibn Isa al-Ijli, hadith transmitter

narrators.ambiguity_note: The kunya suggests a specific Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Ali, but the double Ali patronymic creates ambiguity with other narrators of similar names
Several narrators bear the name Muhammad ibn al-Salt; identification based on the chain context in al-Amali al-Saduq
Could refer to Muhammad ibn Bukayr ibn A'yan or other narrators of similar name; the specific identity depends on the generation of the chain

narrators.known_identity: Abbad ibn Abbad ibn Habib al-Muhalabi (d. 181 AH), Basran hadith transmitter

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Sa'd ibn Abdullah al-Ash'ari al-Qummi (d. 301 AH), a prominent Qummi narrator, but the bare name allows for other possibilities
A less commonly cited narrator; identified based on the name as given in the chain

narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn Zayd ibn Jud'an al-Taymi (d. 131 AH), Basran tabi'i narrator
Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib (d. 94 AH), leading Medinan tabi'i scholar and jurist
Abd al-Rahman ibn Samura ibn Habib al-Qurashi (d. 50 AH), companion of the Prophet

related_quran.context: Death and the desire to return - the inevitability of death and the futility of requesting return to life
Continuation of the rejection of return after death - a barrier (barzakh) prevents return until resurrection

key_phrases.phrase_en: There is no god but Allah

similar_content_hints.description: A similar hadith about the Prophet's vision of the afterlife and how deeds take physical form to aid people is found in many Shia and Sunni collections, often with variations in the number and type of scenes described.
The concept of good deeds interceding for their doer at death and in the grave appears in numerous hadiths about the punishment and blessings of the grave.
Hadiths enumerating the virtues of specific acts of worship and their rewards in the Hereafter, particularly those mentioning the Sirat bridge and the Scales.
